title: Close ('Z')
text: Close ('Z') Close a table. Description Close the table corresponding to the current uctrl structure and free all resources used for that table. Update the number of open files and open cursors. If the DBMS does not support table closing, return 0 to Uniface. If a file pointer or file handle was stored in the connector workspace in the Open call, it can be used to close the table. From Uniface Preconditions realized by Uniface—when the Close request is called, the connector is logged on to the DBMS and the table corresponding to the current uctrl structure is open. Table 1. Information Available from Uniface Data Structure->Element Description uctrl->TMEnabled Indicates whether the connector is TM-controlled. uctrl->udrv Points to the connector workspace if a workspace was requested in the Info call. uctrl->udrvini Identification of the current logon path. uctrl->uname Points to the table name. To Uniface Table 2. Connector Return Values Value Description 0 Close was successful. The specified table is closed. -3 Exceptional error. -9 I/O server logon error. -16 DBMS reported network error. When the connector request return value is less than 0, an error code must be supplied in uctrl->udrverr . Service Functions Refer the following service functions that are related to Close ('Z') : udfilcnt — Update the number of open files. udcurscnt — Update the number of open cursors. TM-Controlled Connectors Connectors that are TM-controlled do not receive Commit and Rollback requests. Therefore, connectors that normally perform cursor administration at the end of a transaction may not be able to determine whether a particular cursor is open or closed when the connector is TM-controlled. TM-controlled connectors that use the Close request to close cursors may not be able to distinguish between open cursors and those that were closed implicitly by the DBMS at the end of a transaction. These connectors can choose to ignore an error from the DBMS which indicates that a cursor was already closed. TM-controlled connectors are not allowed to begin, commit or rollback transactions. Related Links uctrl Exchanges general information between Uniface and the connector, and describes a table. TM-Controlled DBMS Connectors A database connector can be controlled by a transaction manager only if it is TM-capable. Uniface does not provide TM-controlled connectors and does not directly support third-party transaction managers. However, you can use the APIs provided by the Database Connector Interface to build a custom database connector that does. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
